nameMeanings.name: Mora
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Jorge, Miguel, Jesus, Francisco, Roberto, Mario, Pedro.  Portuguese Ligia, Lidio.

Spanish and Portuguese; Catalan (also Móra): topographic name for someone who lived where mulberries grew, from mora ‘mulberry’ (from Late Latin mora, originally the plural of Latin morum); or a habitational name from any of the places called (La) Mora, for example in the Spanish provinces of Albacete, León, Navarre, and Toledo. Compare Spanish De la Mora 1 and Lamora .
French (southern); Catalan (Morà): from a short form of the medieval Latin personal name Morandus (see Morand 1).

variants.name: Moras
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Angel, Guadalupe, Manuel, Marina, Rafael, Santiago.
Spanish: habitational name from any of the places called Moras, named with plural of mora ‘mulberry’ (see Mora ).
Galician (Morás): habitational name from Morás in A Coruña.

variants.name: De la Mora
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jorge, Angel, Arturo, Estela, Jesus, Leticia, Manuel, Rafael, Raul, Sergio.
variant, with the preposition de and definite article la ‘from the’, of Mora 1 ‘mulberry’.

variants.name: Lamora
meanings: Spanish: variant of Mora , with fused definite article la.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.
